subject: Live Stream & Record for T/L [DECEASED] at [LOCATION] - [DATE] [TIME]
body_parts: My name is Graham. May I begin by sending you my condolences on the loss of your [RELATION].
[CLIENT REP] at [CLIENT COMPANY] contacted me regarding Live Streaming and Recording the service at [LOCATION] on [DATE] at [TIME].

My business is to record funeral ceremonies and produce fully edited high-definition videos, and as part of this service I also set up a camera for live streaming. I am usually successful but I cannot offer a guarantee as there are factors outside my control. Please see the notes below.

When filming I use up to 4 cameras placed discreetly in the best locations I can find. I aim to have a general view from behind the congregation, then side views from cameras closer to the lectern(s) and altar. When editing I do not remove any part of the service but cut between the cameras to get the most appropriate view, and use digital zoom while editing to get closer to those speaking. I place a number of sound recorders where they can collect the best sound possible. I do not focus on faces, other than those speaking, or by request.

If you would like to supply photographs, these can be added to the final video as a slideshow, to be placed either during the service or at the end. Likewise, I can include any supplementary video provided. Please ask if you need further details.

In most cases this is successful, but there are 2 situations that can defeat best efforts. One is that the mobile signal inside a church is often quite poor, and most churches do not offer wifi. The other is that streaming uses a lot of data bandwidth, and when a large number of people are present their own mobile phones will be competing for this bandwith even when on mute. Since my live streams do not have priority they can suffer intermittent or even total failure. This problem can also occur where large numbers of phones are connected to the local wifi hotspot. Live streaming relies upon the telephone network and even the BBC has problems with outside broadcast from time to time. It is most reliable at locations that have installed a landline for that exclusive purpose or where a direct satellite link is available. Since neither of these options are available to me, I rely on either the mobile phone network or wifi.
Whether or not the live stream was successful, my camera will still save the streaming video to its own internal memory. I will post this at the original link a few hours after the service.
I always test as close as possible to the start of the service whether a stream can be supported, but this does not ensure that when everyone arrives it will continue to work.
I have prepared a link (see the top of this message) for you to send to all those who you wish to invite to view the service, which usually goes live about 15 minutes before the service starts.
If you wish to send photographs, please do email them to me (at: tech@topcelebrant.com). If the files are large, it may be easier to send via a platform such as: https://wetransfer.com.
I always aim to arrive at the church at least an hour before the service.
If you have any further questions, any at all, please do not hesitate to contact me by phone, email or WhatsApp.
